tested_steps: Ran `make test` and confirmed 1512 unit tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed 11 smoke tests passed.
Ran `make db-push` against RDS Postgres and confirmed Drizzle completed with `No changes detected`.

Verified the live database contains the expected public tables: organizations, user, org_memberships, projects, deployments, pages, api_keys, agent_jobs, assistant_conversations, analytics_events, and audit_logs.

Inserted parent and child records in a transaction, deleted the organization, and confirmed cascading deletes removed memberships, projects, pages, deployments, API keys, agent jobs, assistant conversations, analytics events, and audit logs.
Verified unique indexes reject duplicate organization slugs and duplicate org memberships with Postgres error code `23505`.
Verified enum columns reject invalid `organizations.plan` and `deployments.status` values with Postgres error code `22P02`.

Verified default JSON/JSONB values are correct in practice: organizations.settings `{}`, projects.settings `{}`, pages.frontmatter `{}`, agent_jobs.messages `[]`, assistant_conversations.messages `[]`, analytics_events.data `{}`, and audit_logs.details `{}`.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the full unit suite passed before deeper QA.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed smoke coverage still passed.
Confirmed an unauthenticated `POST /api/uploads/presign` and `GET /api/uploads/presign` returned live S3 presigned URLs, reproducing a storage auth bypass before the fix.
Added route-level auth + org membership enforcement and verified the same unauthenticated POST now returns `401 Unauthorized`.

Generated a real Better Auth session cookie tied to a dedicated QA org/project in Postgres, then requested an authorized upload presign and confirmed the URL encoded a 900 second (`X-Amz-Expires=900`) expiry window.
Uploaded a real text file to S3 via the presigned PUT URL and verified the authorized download presign returned the uploaded `hello infra002` payload.

Verified real bucket CORS with an `OPTIONS` preflight to the presigned URL: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:3015`, methods `GET, PUT, POST`, and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: content-type`.
Requested a 49 MiB upload presign, uploaded the file successfully, and confirmed the stored S3 object content length was `51380224` bytes with content type `video/mp4`.
Requested an upload larger than 50 MiB and verified the app rejected it with `400 Bad Request` and `File exceeds maximum size of 52428800 bytes`.
Requested a presign for a 5 byte upload and attempted to PUT a 10 byte body; S3 rejected it with `403 SignatureDoesNotMatch`, confirming the signed content length is enforced.

Requested two presigned URLs for the same key, uploaded `AAAAAA` and `BBBBBB` concurrently, and verified both writes succeeded with the final stored object resolving to one winner (`BBBBBB` in this run), i.e. last-write-wins overwrite semantics with no object lock.

bugs_found.description: The uploads presign API minted real S3 upload and download URLs for unauthenticated callers and for arbitrary org/project identifiers.
bugs_found.expected: Only authenticated members of the target project's organization should be able to mint upload/download presigned URLs, and only admins/editors should be allowed to upload new assets.
bugs_found.actual: Anonymous requests received fully usable presigned S3 URLs for both PUT and GET operations.
bugs_found.reproduction: POST `http://localhost:3015/api/uploads/presign` with any orgId/projectId/filename/contentType body, or GET `/api/uploads/presign?key=...`, without cookies.

bugs_found.description: The advertised 50 MB upload cap was only enforced in the client and the presigned URL did not bind the upload size.
bugs_found.expected: Oversized uploads should be rejected server-side and the presigned PUT should enforce the expected content length.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the route accepted any size because `size` was not validated and the signed request omitted `content-length`.
bugs_found.reproduction: Request a presigned URL without a size field and upload any body length directly to S3.

fix_description: Locked `/api/uploads/presign` behind authenticated project access, restricted uploads to admin/editor roles, required a `size` field, and signed `Content-Length` so real S3 uploads enforce the 50 MB limit.

tested_steps: Added the missing `react-is` dependency, which was preventing the Next.js dev server from compiling and returning `/login` successfully.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1521 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed 11 smoke tests passed, including the authenticated dashboard, settings, editor, analytics, workflows, and MCP pages.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/login` to verify the real Google OAuth flow with `ashleyha0317@gmail.com`: login page rendered, Google account selection worked, consent completed, and the callback redirected into `/onboarding` for the first-time user path.
Queried Postgres after the real OAuth callback and confirmed Better Auth created `user`, `account`, and `session` records for `ashleyha0317@gmail.com` with provider `google` and a live session row.
Called `/api/auth/get-session` from the authenticated browser session and confirmed the response returned the live Better Auth session and Google user payload.

Refreshed the authenticated onboarding page and confirmed the session persisted; navigating to `/login` and `/dashboard` in the same browser session kept the user authenticated and routed back into the authenticated flow.

Signed out through `POST /api/auth/sign-out`, confirmed `/api/auth/get-session` returned `null`, confirmed the session row was removed from Postgres, and confirmed `/dashboard` redirected back to `/login?returnTo=%2Fdashboard`.
Verified the test-only `POST /api/test/create-session` route returned `404 Not Found` in normal dev mode, so the Playwright auth helper remains guarded outside test runs.

bugs_found.description: The app could not compile in dev because `recharts` imported `react-is`, but `react-is` was not installed.
bugs_found.expected: Auth pages should load successfully so the Google OAuth flow can be exercised end to end.
bugs_found.actual: Navigating to `/login` returned a Next.js `500` error caused by `Module not found: Can't resolve 'react-is'` from `src/app/analytics/page.tsx`.
bugs_found.reproduction: Start the dev server with `npm run dev` and request `http://localhost:3015/login`; Next.js fails compilation because `recharts` cannot resolve `react-is`.

fix_description: Installed the missing `react-is` dependency, then re-ran auth QA to verify real Google OAuth, DB-backed session creation, persistence, sign-out, protected-route redirects, and the test-only session route guard.

tested_steps: Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1521 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed the 11 smoke tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` and confirmed 12 onboarding tests passed, including empty-slug validation, existing-org redirect, and concurrent org creation.

Used Ever CLI against `http://localhost:3015/login` with the real Google OAuth account `ashleyha0317@gmail.com`; Google redirected back into the clone and, with no org membership present, the authenticated user landed on `/onboarding`.

Completed the live onboarding flow in Ever: created organization `Auth 002 QA Org`, skipped the optional GitHub step, created project `Auth 002 QA Project`, reached the success screen, and then landed on the dashboard.

Queried Postgres after the live flow and confirmed the real OAuth user had an `admin` membership for org slug `auth-002-qa-org`, plus project slug `auth-002-qa-project` with subdomain `auth-002-qa-org-auth-002-qa-project`.
Navigated the authenticated browser back to `/onboarding` after org creation and confirmed it redirected to `/dashboard`, satisfying the existing-org redirect path.

Started a temporary `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true` dev server, created two isolated authenticated test sessions through `/api/test/create-session`, posted the same org name twice to `/api/orgs`, and confirmed slug collision handling returned `slug-collision-org` and `slug-collision-org-mnqomz3k`.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1521 tests before deeper QA.
Ran the smoke suite against the active dev server and confirmed all 11 smoke tests passed.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/settings/organization/api-keys` with the real Google-authenticated session to verify the page renders, create an assistant key through the modal, confirm the one-time reveal, confirm copy-to-clipboard flips to `Copied!`, and confirm revoke removes the key from the list. Saved screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-created-key.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-after-revoke.jpg`.

Created a real admin API key and a real assistant API key for org `auth-002-qa-org`, then verified the admin key successfully authenticated `POST /api/v1/project/update/3c9de81a-cca7-4673-aaf5-29bc69aea5f7` with `201 Created` and a deployment status id.

Verified assistant-route auth against the live server: before the fix, `POST /api/v1/assistant/search` returned `500 Internal Server Error` for both a valid assistant key and a valid admin key; after the fix, the same assistant key returned `200 OK` and the admin key returned `403 Forbidden â€” assistant API key required`.
Queried Postgres after the live API requests and confirmed `api_keys.last_used_at` updated for the keys that successfully authenticated.

Demoted the real org membership in Postgres from `admin` to `editor` and then `viewer`, and from the still-authenticated browser session verified both roles received `403 Forbidden â€” admin role required` on `POST /api/api-keys` and `DELETE /api/api-keys/:id`. Restored the membership to `admin` afterward.

Issued 10 concurrent `POST /api/api-keys` requests in the same org and confirmed all 10 returned `201 Created` with distinct `mint_` key prefixes, so no collision or race failure occurred in this run.
Cleaned up the QA-created API keys from the live org after verification.
Added authenticated Playwright coverage for the assistant-key auth contract and re-ran `tests/e2e/api-keys.spec.ts`, which passed 7/7 after updating stale assertions to match the current UI contract.
Ran `make check && make test` after the fix and confirmed typecheck, lint/format, and the full unit suite passed.

bugs_found.description: Assistant API routes returned `500 Internal Server Error` for valid assistant keys instead of serving the request.
bugs_found.expected: A valid `mint_dsc_` assistant key should authenticate assistant routes and return a normal JSON response, even when the search result set is empty.
bugs_found.actual: A real `POST /api/v1/assistant/search` with a valid assistant key updated `last_used_at` but failed with an empty `500` response.
bugs_found.reproduction: Create a real assistant API key from `/settings/organization/api-keys`, then `curl -X POST http://localhost:3015/api/v1/assistant/search -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assistant-key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"getting started","pageSize":3}'`.

bugs_found.description: Assistant-only API routes accepted admin keys.
bugs_found.expected: Routes documented as requiring assistant API keys should reject `mint_` admin keys with `403 Forbidden`.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, a real `mint_` admin key authenticated assistant routes and reached the route logic instead of being rejected.
bugs_found.reproduction: Create a real admin API key from `/settings/organization/api-keys`, then `curl -X POST http://localhost:3015/api/v1/assistant/search -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin-key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"getting started","pageSize":3}'`.

fix_description: Replaced the assistant-route project scoping query with `inArray(...)`, enforced assistant-only key type checks across the assistant v1 routes, and tightened the API-key E2E coverage to exercise real assistant/admin bearer-key behavior.
